query: "Attention Is All You Need" arXiv first posted year
answer: "Attention Is All You Need" was first posted on arXiv in 2017. The paper introduced the Transformer model. It revolutionized natural language processing.

results.title: Today 6 years ago, “Attention is All You Need” went on Arxiv! Happy birthday Transformer! 🎂 Fun facts: - Transformer did not invent attention, but pushed it to the extreme. The first attention… | Jim Fan | 12 comments
results.snippet: The first attention paper was published 3 years prior (2014) and had an unassuming title: “Neural Machine Translation by Jointly Learning to Align and Translate”, from Yoshua Bengio’s lab. With a great respect to the Transformer, "It’s remarkable that AGI (some day soon) can trace its origin to the humble Google Translate" isn't true. It's kind of amazing 6 years later the basic transformer block of 1 attention + 2 MLP layer still holds? Transformative in more ways than one, the paper not only pushed attention to its limits but paved the way for much of the NLP research to follow. As Nikolay Varankin rightly points out, AGI is vastly different from the Transformer, but as AI continues to inch forward, it's exciting to think about what the future holds.

results.title: Attention Is All You Need https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.03762 It's from 2017 but I ... | Hacker News
results.snippet: |     |  |  | | --- | --- | | |  | | --- | | tehsauce on Dec 8, 2020  | next )   “it can detect patterns among an O(n) input size without requiring an O(n^2) size neural net” This might be misleading, the amount of computation for processing a sequence size N with a vanilla transformer is still N^2. Here's an annotated version, with code, which helps:  | | | |  | | --- | | 6gvONxR4sf7o on Dec 8, 2020  | parent | prev | next )   It's crazy to me to see what still feel like new developments (come on, it was just 2017!) making their way into mainstream general purpose undergraduate textbooks like AIMA. | | | |  | | --- | | mrfox321 on Dec 8, 2020  | prev )   I would argue that input scaling is not fundamental to Transformers.

results.title: The first neural attention mechanism that actually works in practice
results.snippet: The paper Attention Is All You Need was posted of arXiv a bit more than 3 years ago. The first paper that proposed a neural attention
